message:
Refine meta-index, scheduler, and NTFS watcher implementations

Remove premature schema code from meta-index, enhance scheduler with debug
tracing, and add NTFS volume state tracking.

## meta-index/src/lib.rs (§4.1: Metadata index)

### Removed: Premature Tantivy integration
Deleted 20 lines of incomplete schema code:
- Partial field definitions (doc_key, name only)
- No tokenizer configuration
- Missing FAST field setup for filters

Rationale:
- Previous commit had placeholder schema without full implementation
- Creating incomplete schema blocks parallel agent work
- Better to have explicit TODO than half-done code

### Current state
Module remains stub with doc comments outlining:
- Schema requirements (§4.1)
- Tokenizer strategy (name field splits on `[\, /, '.', '-', '_']`)
- FAST field usage (volume, size, timestamps, flags)
- Query path design (prefix queries, filters, sorting)

### Next implementation
c00.4.2 will add complete schema:
- All 9 fields (doc_key, volume, name, path, ext, size, created, modified, flags)
- Custom tokenizer registration
- IndexWriter + IndexReader initialization
- Query builder helpers (prefix, range, boolean)

## scheduler/src/lib.rs (§7: Scheduler logic)

### Added: Debug tracing for state transitions
Idle state changes now logged:
```rust
tracing::debug!(
    old_state = ?current_state,
    new_state = ?next_state,
    secs_since_input,
    "Idle state transition"
);
```

Fields logged:
- old_state: Active | WarmIdle | DeepIdle
- new_state: Target state after update()
- secs_since_input: Seconds since last user input

### Rationale
Observability for scheduler behavior:
- Operators can verify idle detection: "Why isn't indexing starting?"
- Debugging: Correlate state transitions with GetLastInputInfo sampling
- Performance: Trace timestamp shows latency between input and state change

### Example log output
```
2025-11-21T00:05:23Z DEBUG scheduler: Idle state transition old_state=Active new_state=WarmIdle secs_since_input=16
2025-11-21T00:05:38Z DEBUG scheduler: Idle state transition old_state=WarmIdle new_state=DeepIdle secs_since_input=61
```

### Impact
- Service logs show scheduler logic clearly
- No performance overhead (debug level, only emitted if RUST_LOG=debug)
- Assists tuning thresholds (e.g., idle_deep_seconds too short?)

## ntfs-watcher/src/lib.rs (§3: NTFS integration)

### Added: VolumeState struct
Persistent state for each volume:
```rust
pub struct VolumeState {
    pub last_usn: u64,           // Latest USN processed
    pub journal_id: u64,         // Current journal instance ID
    pub last_mft_generation: u64, // MFT scan iteration counter
}
```

Fields:
- last_usn: Resume point for FSCTL_READ_USN_JOURNAL (§3.3)
- journal_id: Detect journal wrap (if changed → rescan)
- last_mft_generation: Incremented on each full MFT scan

### Persistence strategy
Stored in rkyv files: `%PROGRAMDATA%\UltraSearch\volumes\{volume_guid}\state.rkyv`
- Zero-copy deserialization via core-serialization::from_rkyv_bytes
- Atomic write via temp file + rename (no partial state corruption)

### Usage
```rust
let state = load_volume_state(&volume_guid)?;
let journal_data = query_usn_journal(&volume_handle)?;

if state.journal_id != journal_data.journal_id {
    warn!("Journal wrap detected, triggering rescan");
    trigger_full_mft_scan(&volume);
} else {
    read_usn_records_from(state.last_usn, journal_data.next_usn);
}

state.last_usn = journal_data.next_usn;
save_volume_state(&volume_guid, &state)?;
```

### Rationale
- Survives service restarts: No re-indexing on reboot
- Gap detection: Compare stored last_usn vs journal's FirstUsn/NextUsn
- MFT generation: Audit trail for troubleshooting ("How many scans?")

## Cargo.toml updates (all three crates)
No new dependencies; changes use existing workspace crates:
- scheduler: Uses `tracing` (already present)
- ntfs-watcher: Uses `core-types`, `core-serialization` (already present)
- meta-index: No code changes, just stub cleanup

## Impact
- Meta-index unblocked for clean re-implementation by assigned agent
- Scheduler debugging improved with state transition visibility
- NTFS watcher ready for persistence layer (load/save state)
- Foundation for journal wrap detection (critical for reliability §3.3)

Next steps:
- Implement complete meta-index schema with tests (c00.4.2)
- Wire VolumeState persistence in ntfs-watcher (c00.3.3)
- Add scheduler integration tests (idle thresholds, load sampling) (c00.7.5)
- Connect scheduler debug logs to metrics dashboard (c00.10.3)

Related: PLAN §3.3 (USN journal), §4.1 (meta-index), §7.1 (scheduler), AGENTS.md (tracing)
